title: etlingera littoralis
intro: Etlingera littoralis, also known as the coastal ginger or littoral etlingera, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical coastal regions in Asia and Australia. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Etlingera littoralis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as possessing antioxidant properties that help protect against cell damage. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
